title: The end of Spring: India Jumps Straight from Winter to Summer
what_talking_about: Instead of spring, summer arrived overnight. Mumbai was already at 40°C and Shimla 25°C in March.
why_care: Food prices may go up: Wheat needs cool weather before harvest. This year’s heat hit Punjab and Haryana’s crop at exactly that stage, and farmers are predicting a weaker harvest.

An El Nino may be coming: Scientists are predicting there’s an 80% chance of El Niño this year, which usually means higher than average temperatures and a weaker monsoon. Dry winter + weak monsoon = water shortages.

sources.title: Down To Earth: It feels as if the spring never arrived
The Statesman: Climate Change Overrides La Niña, Driving Early Heatwaves and a Shrinking Indian Winter
Down To Earth: Death of winter — Heat arrives early, threatens to shrink Punjab and Haryana’s wheat harvest
